libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep/configure the test JVM with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` when exercising OGNL runtime reflection and access-handler behavior. Do not add a separate Javassist dependency unless Gradle resolution proves the transitive `org.javassist:javassist` dependency is absent. If covering OGNL's optional security/access modes, set and restore properties such as `ognl.security.manager`, `ognl.UseJDK9PlusAccessHandler`, or `ognl.UseFirstMatchGetSetLookup` inside narrowly scoped tests; properties read during `OgnlRuntime` initialization may require isolated class loading or a forked JVM.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: SecurityManager-based OGNL paths are deprecated/disabled on newer JDKs and may fail on JDK 25 if tests assume `System.setSecurityManager` succeeds.
Several OGNL runtime flags are static initialization-time properties, so test order or prior class loading can make property-based assertions flaky.
Deep reflection/access-handler coverage is JDK-version-sensitive and remains subject to local Gradle and native-image verification.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL has no external service requirement and its Javassist dependency is transitive, but meaningful tests often exercise deep reflection/JDK-access paths that need JVM/test setup already present in the existing scaffold.
